name: Royal Leamington Spa, England
significance: Crowley's birthplace and the opening setting of his family story.

name: Boleskine House, Loch Ness
significance: Site of Crowley's Abramelin work and one of the signature places in his magical biography.

name: London, England
significance: Base for the Golden Dawn, publishing, and much of Crowley's later public life.

name: Cairo, Egypt
significance: Site of the Cairo Working and the reception narrative for Liber AL vel Legis.

name: Bou Saada, Algeria
significance: Desert location for the Enochian Aethyr work with Victor Neuburg.

name: Paris, France
significance: Recurring base for writing, exile, publishing, and the later French connection.

name: Cefalu, Sicily
significance: Location of the Abbey of Thelema and a major Thelemic experiment.

name: New York City, USA
significance: Important for Crowley's American lectures, wartime exile, and occult circle.

name: Hastings, England
significance: Crowley's final home and place of death.

name: Blythe Road, London
significance: Associated with Golden Dawn administrative history and occult organizational conflict.

name: Redhill, Surrey, England
significance: Family home base after Crowley's childhood shift out of Leamington.

name: Cambridge, England
significance: Site of Trinity College and Crowley's undergraduate formation.

name: Schonbuhl Glacier, Swiss Alps
significance: Crowley's alpine climbing terrain and one of the symbolic sources of his self-making.

name: Karakoram / K2
significance: The mountain expedition zone Crowley used to test stamina, will, and fellowship.

name: Darjeeling, India
significance: Base for Himalayan training and the eastern travel current in Crowley's life.

name: Tunis, Tunisia
significance: Crowley's North African exile after the Abbey collapsed.

name: Berlin, Germany
significance: Key site for the Weimar-era artistic and social network around Crowley.

name: Oxford, England
significance: Site of key student, literary, and scandalous social encounters around Crowley's circle.

name: Caxton Hall, London
significance: Performance venue for the Rites of Eleusis.

name: Pasadena, California
significance: American occult afterlife territory connected to Jack Parsons and the Agape Lodge.
